title: The Future of UI Design is Agentic Design
description: Explores how AI agents like Anima are fundamentally changing the design process, shifting from traditional workflows to human-AI co-creation. This deep dive reveals the practical implications of agentic design tools and why designers need to adapt their approach in 2026.

title: Design and engineering, as one
description: Challenges the outdated organizational model that separates design and engineering, arguing most teams are solving yesterday's problems with today's structure. This thought-provoking piece calls for reimagining how product teams collaborate in a modern context.

title: How to turn your competitor's worst reviews into your strongest design argument
description: Provides a practical framework for using competitive research as evidence-backed design justification rather than just visual inspiration. A tactical guide that helps designers win stakeholder buy-in through data-driven competitive analysis.

title: Squash and stretch
description: Applies Disney's classic animation principles to modern SVG micro-interactions, showing designers how subtle motion techniques make digital interfaces feel more natural and delightful. A hands-on exploration of turning animation theory into practical web design.

title: Handmade designs: the new trust signal
description: Identifies a growing user preference for human-crafted design in an AI-saturated landscape, positioning authenticity as a competitive advantage. This insights piece explains why showing intentional, human design choices is becoming crucial for brand trust.
